41	// Upgrade is the action for upgrading releases.
42	//
43	// It provides the implementation of 'helm upgrade'.
44	type Upgrade struct {
45		cfg *Configuration
46	
47		ChartPathOptions
48	
49		// Install is a purely informative flag that indicates whether this upgrade was done in "install" mode.
50		//
51		// Applications may use this to determine whether this Upgrade operation was done as part of a
52		// pure upgrade (Upgrade.Install == false) or as part of an install-or-upgrade operation
53		// (Upgrade.Install == true).
54		//
55		// Setting this to `true` will NOT cause `Upgrade` to perform an install if the release does not exist.
56		// That process must be handled by creating an Install action directly. See cmd/upgrade.go for an
57		// example of how this flag is used.
58		Install bool
59		// Devel indicates that the operation is done in devel mode.
60		Devel bool
61		// Namespace is the namespace in which this operation should be performed.
62		Namespace string
63		// SkipCRDs skips installing CRDs when install flag is enabled during upgrade
64		SkipCRDs bool
65		// Timeout is the timeout for this operation
66		Timeout time.Duration
67		// Wait determines whether the wait operation should be performed after the upgrade is requested.
68		Wait bool
69		// WaitForJobs determines whether the wait operation for the Jobs should be performed after the upgrade is requested.
70		WaitForJobs bool
71		// DisableHooks disables hook processing if set to true.
72		DisableHooks bool
73		// DryRun controls whether the operation is prepared, but not executed.
74		DryRun bool
75		// DryRunOption controls whether the operation is prepared, but not executed with options on whether or not to interact with the remote cluster.
76		DryRunOption string
77		// HideSecret can be set to true when DryRun is enabled in order to hide
78		// Kubernetes Secrets in the output. It cannot be used outside of DryRun.
79		HideSecret bool
80		// Force will, if set to `true`, ignore certain warnings and perform the upgrade anyway.
81		//
82		// This should be used with caution.
83		Force bool
84		// ResetValues will reset the values to the chart's built-ins rather than merging with existing.
85		ResetValues bool
86		// ReuseValues will re-use the user's last supplied values.
87		ReuseValues bool
88		// ResetThenReuseValues will reset the values to the chart's built-ins then merge with user's last supplied values.
89		ResetThenReuseValues bool
90		// Recreate will (if true) recreate pods after a rollback.
91		Recreate bool
92		// MaxHistory limits the maximum number of revisions saved per release
93		MaxHistory int
94		// Atomic, if true, will roll back on failure.
95		Atomic bool
96		// CleanupOnFail will, if true, cause the upgrade to delete newly-created resources on a failed update.
97		CleanupOnFail bool
98		// SubNotes determines whether sub-notes are rendered in the chart.
99		SubNotes bool
100		// HideNotes determines whether notes are output during upgrade
101		HideNotes bool
102		// SkipSchemaValidation determines if JSON schema validation is disabled.
103		SkipSchemaValidation bool
104		// Description is the description of this operation
105		Description string
106		Labels      map[string]string
107		// PostRender is an optional post-renderer
108		//
109		// If this is non-nil, then after templates are rendered, they will be sent to the
110		// post renderer before sending to the Kubernetes API server.
111		PostRenderer postrender.PostRenderer
112		// DisableOpenAPIValidation controls whether OpenAPI validation is enforced.
113		DisableOpenAPIValidation bool
114		// Get missing dependencies
115		DependencyUpdate bool
116		// Lock to control raceconditions when the process receives a SIGTERM
117		Lock sync.Mutex
118		// Enable DNS lookups when rendering templates
119		EnableDNS bool
120		// TakeOwnership will skip the check for helm annotations and adopt all existing resources.
121		TakeOwnership bool
122	}
123	
124	type resultMessage struct {
125		r *release.Release
126		e error
127	}
128	
129	// NewUpgrade creates a new Upgrade object with the given configuration.
130	func NewUpgrade(cfg *Configuration) *Upgrade {
131		up := &Upgrade{
132			cfg: cfg,
133		}
134		up.ChartPathOptions.registryClient = cfg.RegistryClient
135	
136		return up
137	}
138	
139	// SetRegistryClient sets the registry client to use when fetching charts.
140	func (u *Upgrade) SetRegistryClient(client *registry.Client) {
141		u.ChartPathOptions.registryClient = client
142	}
143	
144	// Run executes the upgrade on the given release.
145	func (u *Upgrade) Run(name string, chart *chart.Chart, vals map[string]interface{}) (*release.Release, error) {
146		ctx := context.Background()
147		return u.RunWithContext(ctx, name, chart, vals)
148	}
149	
150	// RunWithContext executes the upgrade on the given release with context.
151	func (u *Upgrade) RunWithContext(ctx context.Context, name string, chart *chart.Chart, vals map[string]interface{}) (*release.Release, error) {
152		if err := u.cfg.KubeClient.IsReachable(); err != nil {
153			return nil, err
154		}
155	
156		// Make sure if Atomic is set, that wait is set as well. This makes it so
157		// the user doesn't have to specify both
158		u.Wait = u.Wait || u.Atomic
159	
160		if err := chartutil.ValidateReleaseName(name); err != nil {
161			return nil, errors.Errorf("release name is invalid: %s", name)
162		}
163	
164		u.cfg.Log("preparing upgrade for %s", name)
165		currentRelease, upgradedRelease, err := u.prepareUpgrade(name, chart, vals)
166		if err != nil {
167			return nil, err
168		}
169	
170		u.cfg.Releases.MaxHistory = u.MaxHistory
171	
172		u.cfg.Log("performing update for %s", name)
173		res, err := u.performUpgrade(ctx, currentRelease, upgradedRelease)
174		if err != nil {
175			return res, err
176		}
177	
178		// Do not update for dry runs
179		if !u.isDryRun() {
180			u.cfg.Log("updating status for upgraded release for %s", name)
181			if err := u.cfg.Releases.Update(upgradedRelease); err != nil {
182				return res, err
183			}
184		}
185	
186		return res, nil
187	}
188	
189	// isDryRun returns true if Upgrade is set to run as a DryRun
190	func (u *Upgrade) isDryRun() bool {
191		if u.DryRun || u.DryRunOption == "client" || u.DryRunOption == "server" || u.DryRunOption == "true" {
192			return true
193		}
194		return false
195	}
196	
197	// prepareUpgrade builds an upgraded release for an upgrade operation.
198	func (u *Upgrade) prepareUpgrade(name string, chart *chart.Chart, vals map[string]interface{}) (*release.Release, *release.Release, error) {
199		if chart == nil {
200			return nil, nil, errMissingChart
201		}
202	
203		// HideSecret must be used with dry run. Otherwise, return an error.
204		if !u.isDryRun() && u.HideSecret {
205			return nil, nil, errors.New("Hiding Kubernetes secrets requires a dry-run mode")
206		}
207	
208		// finds the last non-deleted release with the given name
209		lastRelease, err := u.cfg.Releases.Last(name)
210		if err != nil {
211			// to keep existing behavior of returning the "%q has no deployed releases" error when an existing release does not exist
212			if errors.Is(err, driver.ErrReleaseNotFound) {
213				return nil, nil, driver.NewErrNoDeployedReleases(name)
214			}
215			return nil, nil, err
216		}
217	
218		// Concurrent `helm upgrade`s will either fail here with `errPending` or when creating the release with "already exists". This should act as a pessimistic lock.
219		if lastRelease.Info.Status.IsPending() {
220			return nil, nil, errPending
221		}
222	
223		var currentRelease *release.Release
224		if lastRelease.Info.Status == release.StatusDeployed {
225			// no need to retrieve the last deployed release from storage as the last release is deployed
226			currentRelease = lastRelease
227		} else {
228			// finds the deployed release with the given name
229			currentRelease, err = u.cfg.Releases.Deployed(name)
230			if err != nil {
231				if errors.Is(err, driver.ErrNoDeployedReleases) &&
232					(lastRelease.Info.Status == release.StatusFailed || lastRelease.Info.Status == release.StatusSuperseded) {
233					currentRelease = lastRelease
234				} else {
235					return nil, nil, err
236				}
237			}
238		}
239	
240		// determine if values will be reused
241		vals, err = u.reuseValues(chart, currentRelease, vals)
242		if err != nil {
243			return nil, nil, err
244		}
245	
246		if err := chartutil.ProcessDependenciesWithMerge(chart, vals); err != nil {
247			return nil, nil, err
248		}
249	
250		// Increment revision count. This is passed to templates, and also stored on
251		// the release object.
252		revision := lastRelease.Version + 1
253	
254		options := chartutil.ReleaseOptions{
255			Name:      name,
256			Namespace: currentRelease.Namespace,
257			Revision:  revision,
258			IsUpgrade: true,
259		}
260	
261		caps, err := u.cfg.getCapabilities()
262		if err != nil {
263			return nil, nil, err
264		}
265		valuesToRender, err := chartutil.ToRenderValuesWithSchemaValidation(chart, vals, options, caps, u.SkipSchemaValidation)
266		if err != nil {
267			return nil, nil, err
268		}
269	
270		// Determine whether or not to interact with remote
271		var interactWithRemote bool
272		if !u.isDryRun() || u.DryRunOption == "server" || u.DryRunOption == "none" || u.DryRunOption == "false" {
273			interactWithRemote = true
274		}
275	
276		hooks, manifestDoc, notesTxt, err := u.cfg.renderResources(chart, valuesToRender, "", "", u.SubNotes, false, false, u.PostRenderer, interactWithRemote, u.EnableDNS, u.HideSecret)
277		if err != nil {
278			return nil, nil, err
279		}
280	
281		if driver.ContainsSystemLabels(u.Labels) {
282			return nil, nil, fmt.Errorf("user supplied labels contains system reserved label name. System labels: %+v", driver.GetSystemLabels())
283		}
284	
285		// Store an upgraded release.
286		upgradedRelease := &release.Release{
287			Name:      name,
288			Namespace: currentRelease.Namespace,
289			Chart:     chart,
290			Config:    vals,
291			Info: &release.Info{
292				FirstDeployed: currentRelease.Info.FirstDeployed,
293				LastDeployed:  Timestamper(),
294				Status:        release.StatusPendingUpgrade,
295				Description:   "Preparing upgrade", // This should be overwritten later.
296			},
297			Version:  revision,
298			Manifest: manifestDoc.String(),
299			Hooks:    hooks,
300			Labels:   mergeCustomLabels(lastRelease.Labels, u.Labels),
301		}
302	
303		if len(notesTxt) > 0 {
304			upgradedRelease.Info.Notes = notesTxt
305		}
306		err = validateManifest(u.cfg.KubeClient, manifestDoc.Bytes(), !u.DisableOpenAPIValidation)
307		return currentRelease, upgradedRelease, err
308	}
309	
310	func (u *Upgrade) performUpgrade(ctx context.Context, originalRelease, upgradedRelease *release.Release) (*release.Release, error) {
311		current, err := u.cfg.KubeClient.Build(bytes.NewBufferString(originalRelease.Manifest), false)
312		if err != nil {
313			// Checking for removed Kubernetes API error so can provide a more informative error message to the user
314			// Ref: https://github.com/helm/helm/issues/7219
315			if strings.Contains(err.Error(), "unable to recognize \"\": no matches for kind") {
316				return upgradedRelease, errors.Wrap(err, "current release manifest contains removed kubernetes api(s) for this "+
317					"kubernetes version and it is therefore unable to build the kubernetes "+
318					"objects for performing the diff. error from kubernetes")
319			}
320			return upgradedRelease, errors.Wrap(err, "unable to build kubernetes objects from current release manifest")
321		}
322		target, err := u.cfg.KubeClient.Build(bytes.NewBufferString(upgradedRelease.Manifest), !u.DisableOpenAPIValidation)
323		if err != nil {
324			return upgradedRelease, errors.Wrap(err, "unable to build kubernetes objects from new release manifest")
325		}
326	
327		// It is safe to use force only on target because these are resources currently rendered by the chart.
328		err = target.Visit(setMetadataVisitor(upgradedRelease.Name, upgradedRelease.Namespace, true))
329		if err != nil {
330			return upgradedRelease, err
331		}
332	
333		// Do a basic diff using gvk + name to figure out what new resources are being created so we can validate they don't already exist
334		existingResources := make(map[string]bool)
335		for _, r := range current {
336			existingResources[objectKey(r)] = true
337		}
338	
339		var toBeCreated kube.ResourceList
340		for _, r := range target {
341			if !existingResources[objectKey(r)] {
342				toBeCreated = append(toBeCreated, r)
343			}
344		}
345	
346		var toBeUpdated kube.ResourceList
347		if u.TakeOwnership {
348			toBeUpdated, err = requireAdoption(toBeCreated)
349		} else {
350			toBeUpdated, err = existingResourceConflict(toBeCreated, upgradedRelease.Name, upgradedRelease.Namespace)
351		}
352		if err != nil {
353			return nil, errors.Wrap(err, "Unable to continue with update")
354		}
355	
356		toBeUpdated.Visit(func(r *resource.Info, err error) error {
357			if err != nil {
358				return err
359			}
360			current.Append(r)
361			return nil
362		})
363	
364		// Run if it is a dry run
365		if u.isDryRun() {
366			u.cfg.Log("dry run for %s", upgradedRelease.Name)
367			if len(u.Description) > 0 {
368				upgradedRelease.Info.Description = u.Description
369			} else {
370				upgradedRelease.Info.Description = "Dry run complete"
371			}
372			return upgradedRelease, nil
373		}
374	
375		u.cfg.Log("creating upgraded release for %s", upgradedRelease.Name)
376		if err := u.cfg.Releases.Create(upgradedRelease); err != nil {
377			return nil, err
378		}
379		rChan := make(chan resultMessage)
380		ctxChan := make(chan resultMessage)
381		doneChan := make(chan interface{})
382		defer close(doneChan)
383		go u.releasingUpgrade(rChan, upgradedRelease, current, target, originalRelease)
384		go u.handleContext(ctx, doneChan, ctxChan, upgradedRelease)
385		select {
386		case result := <-rChan:
387			return result.r, result.e
388		case result := <-ctxChan:
389			return result.r, result.e
390		}
391	}
392	
393	// Function used to lock the Mutex, this is important for the case when the atomic flag is set.
394	// In that case the upgrade will finish before the rollback is finished so it is necessary to wait for the rollback to finish.
395	// The rollback will be trigger by the function failRelease
396	func (u *Upgrade) reportToPerformUpgrade(c chan<- resultMessage, rel *release.Release, created kube.ResourceList, err error) {
397		u.Lock.Lock()
398		if err != nil {
399			rel, err = u.failRelease(rel, created, err)
400		}
401		c <- resultMessage{r: rel, e: err}
402		u.Lock.Unlock()
403	}
404	
405	// Setup listener for SIGINT and SIGTERM
406	func (u *Upgrade) handleContext(ctx context.Context, done chan interface{}, c chan<- resultMessage, upgradedRelease *release.Release) {
407		select {
408		case <-ctx.Done():
409			err := ctx.Err()
410	
411			// when the atomic flag is set the ongoing release finish first and doesn't give time for the rollback happens.
412			u.reportToPerformUpgrade(c, upgradedRelease, kube.ResourceList{}, err)
413		case <-done:
414			return
415		}
416	}
417	func (u *Upgrade) releasingUpgrade(c chan<- resultMessage, upgradedRelease *release.Release, current kube.ResourceList, target kube.ResourceList, originalRelease *release.Release) {
418		// pre-upgrade hooks
419	
420		if !u.DisableHooks {
421			if err := u.cfg.execHook(upgradedRelease, release.HookPreUpgrade, u.Timeout); err != nil {
422				u.reportToPerformUpgrade(c, upgradedRelease, kube.ResourceList{}, fmt.Errorf("pre-upgrade hooks failed: %s", err))
423				return
424			}
425		} else {
426			u.cfg.Log("upgrade hooks disabled for %s", upgradedRelease.Name)
427		}
428	
429		results, err := u.cfg.KubeClient.Update(current, target, u.Force)
430		if err != nil {
431			u.cfg.recordRelease(originalRelease)
432			u.reportToPerformUpgrade(c, upgradedRelease, results.Created, err)
433			return
434		}
435	
436		if u.Recreate {
437			// NOTE: Because this is not critical for a release to succeed, we just
438			// log if an error occurs and continue onward. If we ever introduce log
439			// levels, we should make these error level logs so users are notified
440			// that they'll need to go do the cleanup on their own
441			if err := recreate(u.cfg, results.Updated); err != nil {
442				u.cfg.Log(err.Error())
443			}
444		}
445	
446		if u.Wait {
447			u.cfg.Log(
448				"waiting for release %s resources (created: %d updated: %d  deleted: %d)",
449				upgradedRelease.Name, len(results.Created), len(results.Updated), len(results.Deleted))
450			if u.WaitForJobs {
451				if err := u.cfg.KubeClient.WaitWithJobs(target, u.Timeout); err != nil {
452					u.cfg.recordRelease(originalRelease)
453					u.reportToPerformUpgrade(c, upgradedRelease, results.Created, err)
454					return
455				}
456			} else {
457				if err := u.cfg.KubeClient.Wait(target, u.Timeout); err != nil {
458					u.cfg.recordRelease(originalRelease)
459					u.reportToPerformUpgrade(c, upgradedRelease, results.Created, err)
460					return
461				}
462			}
463		}
464	
465		// post-upgrade hooks
466		if !u.DisableHooks {
467			if err := u.cfg.execHook(upgradedRelease, release.HookPostUpgrade, u.Timeout); err != nil {
468				u.reportToPerformUpgrade(c, upgradedRelease, results.Created, fmt.Errorf("post-upgrade hooks failed: %s", err))
469				return
470			}
471		}
472	
473		originalRelease.Info.Status = release.StatusSuperseded
474		u.cfg.recordRelease(originalRelease)
475	
476		upgradedRelease.Info.Status = release.StatusDeployed
477		if len(u.Description) > 0 {
478			upgradedRelease.Info.Description = u.Description
479		} else {
480			upgradedRelease.Info.Description = "Upgrade complete"
481		}
482		u.reportToPerformUpgrade(c, upgradedRelease, nil, nil)
483	}

485	func (u *Upgrade) failRelease(rel *release.Release, created kube.ResourceList, err error) (*release.Release, error) {
486		msg := fmt.Sprintf("Upgrade %q failed: %s", rel.Name, err)
487		u.cfg.Log("warning: %s", msg)
488	
489		rel.Info.Status = release.StatusFailed
490		rel.Info.Description = msg
491		u.cfg.recordRelease(rel)
492		if u.CleanupOnFail && len(created) > 0 {
493			u.cfg.Log("Cleanup on fail set, cleaning up %d resources", len(created))
494			_, errs := u.cfg.KubeClient.Delete(created)
495			if errs != nil {
496				var errorList []string
497				for _, e := range errs {
498					errorList = append(errorList, e.Error())
499				}
500				return rel, errors.Wrapf(fmt.Errorf("unable to cleanup resources: %s", strings.Join(errorList, ", ")), "an error occurred while cleaning up resources. original upgrade error: %s", err)
501			}
502			u.cfg.Log("Resource cleanup complete")
503		}
504		if u.Atomic {
505			u.cfg.Log("Upgrade failed and atomic is set, rolling back to last successful release")
506	
507			// As a protection, get the last successful release before rollback.
508			// If there are no successful releases, bail out
509			hist := NewHistory(u.cfg)
510			fullHistory, herr := hist.Run(rel.Name)
511			if herr != nil {
512				return rel, errors.Wrapf(herr, "an error occurred while finding last successful release. original upgrade error: %s", err)
513			}
514	
515			// There isn't a way to tell if a previous release was successful, but
516			// generally failed releases do not get superseded unless the next
517			// release is successful, so this should be relatively safe
518			filteredHistory := releaseutil.FilterFunc(func(r *release.Release) bool {
519				return r.Info.Status == release.StatusSuperseded || r.Info.Status == release.StatusDeployed
520			}).Filter(fullHistory)
521			if len(filteredHistory) == 0 {
522				return rel, errors.Wrap(err, "unable to find a previously successful release when attempting to rollback. original upgrade error")
523			}
524	
525			releaseutil.Reverse(filteredHistory, releaseutil.SortByRevision)
526	
527			rollin := NewRollback(u.cfg)
528			rollin.Version = filteredHistory[0].Version
529			rollin.Wait = true
530			rollin.WaitForJobs = u.WaitForJobs
531			rollin.DisableHooks = u.DisableHooks
532			rollin.Recreate = u.Recreate
533			rollin.Force = u.Force
534			rollin.Timeout = u.Timeout
535			if rollErr := rollin.Run(rel.Name); rollErr != nil {
536				return rel, errors.Wrapf(rollErr, "an error occurred while rolling back the release. original upgrade error: %s", err)
537			}
538			return rel, errors.Wrapf(err, "release %s failed, and has been rolled back due to atomic being set", rel.Name)
539		}
540	
541		return rel, err
542	}
543	
544	// reuseValues copies values from the current release to a new release if the
545	// new release does not have any values.
546	//
547	// If the request already has values, or if there are no values in the current
548	// release, this does nothing.
549	//
550	// This is skipped if the u.ResetValues flag is set, in which case the
551	// request values are not altered.
552	func (u *Upgrade) reuseValues(chart *chart.Chart, current *release.Release, newVals map[string]interface{}) (map[string]interface{}, error) {
553		if u.ResetValues {
554			// If ResetValues is set, we completely ignore current.Config.
555			u.cfg.Log("resetting values to the chart's original version")
556			return newVals, nil
557		}
558	
559		// If the ReuseValues flag is set, we always copy the old values over the new config's values.
560		if u.ReuseValues {
561			u.cfg.Log("reusing the old release's values")
562	
563			// We have to regenerate the old coalesced values:
564			oldVals, err := chartutil.CoalesceValues(current.Chart, current.Config)
565			if err != nil {
566				return nil, errors.Wrap(err, "failed to rebuild old values")
567			}
568	
569			newVals = chartutil.CoalesceTables(newVals, current.Config)
570	
571			chart.Values = oldVals
572	
573			return newVals, nil
574		}
575	
576		// If the ResetThenReuseValues flag is set, we use the new chart's values, but we copy the old config's values over the new config's values.
577		if u.ResetThenReuseValues {
578			u.cfg.Log("merging values from old release to new values")
579	
580			newVals = chartutil.CoalesceTables(newVals, current.Config)
581	
582			return newVals, nil
583		}
584	
585		if len(newVals) == 0 && len(current.Config) > 0 {
586			u.cfg.Log("copying values from %s (v%d) to new release.", current.Name, current.Version)
587			newVals = current.Config
588		}
589		return newVals, nil
590	}

592	func validateManifest(c kube.Interface, manifest []byte, openAPIValidation bool) error {
593		_, err := c.Build(bytes.NewReader(manifest), openAPIValidation)
594		return err
595	}

597	// recreate captures all the logic for recreating pods for both upgrade and
598	// rollback. If we end up refactoring rollback to use upgrade, this can just be
599	// made an unexported method on the upgrade action.
600	func recreate(cfg *Configuration, resources kube.ResourceList) error {
601		for _, res := range resources {
602			versioned := kube.AsVersioned(res)
603			selector, err := kube.SelectorsForObject(versioned)
604			if err != nil {
605				// If no selector is returned, it means this object is
606				// definitely not a pod, so continue onward
607				continue
608			}
609	
610			client, err := cfg.KubernetesClientSet()
611			if err != nil {
612				return errors.Wrapf(err, "unable to recreate pods for object %s/%s because an error occurred", res.Namespace, res.Name)
613			}
614	
615			pods, err := client.CoreV1().Pods(res.Namespace).List(context.Background(), metav1.ListOptions{
616				LabelSelector: selector.String(),
617			})
618			if err != nil {
619				return errors.Wrapf(err, "unable to recreate pods for object %s/%s because an error occurred", res.Namespace, res.Name)
620			}
621	
622			// Restart pods
623			for _, pod := range pods.Items {
624				// Delete each pod for get them restarted with changed spec.
625				if err := client.CoreV1().Pods(pod.Namespace).Delete(context.Background(), pod.Name, *metav1.NewPreconditionDeleteOptions(string(pod.UID))); err != nil {
626					return errors.Wrapf(err, "unable to recreate pods for object %s/%s because an error occurred", res.Namespace, res.Name)
627				}
628			}
629		}
630		return nil
631	}

636	}

47	// waitForResources polls to get the current status of all pods, PVCs, Services and
48	// Jobs(optional) until all are ready or a timeout is reached
49	func (w *waiter) waitForResources(created ResourceList) error {
50		w.log("beginning wait for %d resources with timeout of %v", len(created), w.timeout)
51	
52		startTime := time.Now()
53		ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), w.timeout)
54		defer cancel()
55	
56		numberOfErrors := make([]int, len(created))
57		for i := range numberOfErrors {
58			numberOfErrors[i] = 0
59		}
60	
61		err := wait.PollUntilContextCancel(ctx, 2*time.Second, true, func(ctx context.Context) (bool, error) {
62			waitRetries := 30
63			for i, v := range created {
64				ready, err := w.c.IsReady(ctx, v)
65	
66				if waitRetries > 0 && w.isRetryableError(err, v) {
67					numberOfErrors[i]++
68					if numberOfErrors[i] > waitRetries {
69						w.log("Max number of retries reached")
70						return false, err
71					}
72					w.log("Retrying as current number of retries %d less than max number of retries %d", numberOfErrors[i]-1, waitRetries)
73					return false, nil
74				}
75				numberOfErrors[i] = 0
76				if !ready {
77					return false, err
78				}
79			}
80			return true, nil
81		})
82	
83		elapsed := time.Since(startTime).Round(time.Second)
84		if err != nil {
85			w.log("wait for resources failed after %v: %v", elapsed, err)
86		} else {
87			w.log("wait for resources succeeded within %v", elapsed)
88		}
89	
90		return err
91	}

95			return false
96		}
97		w.log("Error received when checking status of resource %s. Error: '%s', Resource details: '%s'", resource.Name, err, resource)
98		if ev, ok := err.(*apierrors.StatusError); ok {
99			statusCode := ev.Status().Code
100			retryable := w.isRetryableHTTPStatusCode(statusCode)
101			w.log("Status code received: %d. Retryable error? %t", statusCode, retryable)
102			return retryable
103		}
104		w.log("Retryable error? %t", true)
105		return true
106	}
107	
108	func (w *waiter) isRetryableHTTPStatusCode(httpStatusCode int32) bool {
109		return httpStatusCode == 0 || httpStatusCode == http.StatusTooManyRequests || (httpStatusCode >= 500 && httpStatusCode != http.StatusNotImplemented)
110	}

112	// waitForDeletedResources polls to check if all the resources are deleted or a timeout is reached
113	func (w *waiter) waitForDeletedResources(deleted ResourceList) error {
114		w.log("beginning wait for %d resources to be deleted with timeout of %v", len(deleted), w.timeout)
115	
116		ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), w.timeout)
117		defer cancel()
118	
119		return wait.PollUntilContextCancel(ctx, 2*time.Second, true, func(_ context.Context) (bool, error) {
120			for _, v := range deleted {
121				err := v.Get()
122				if err == nil || !apierrors.IsNotFound(err) {
123					return false, err
124				}
125			}
126			return true, nil
127		})
128	}

32	// Rollback is the action for rolling back to a given release.
33	//
34	// It provides the implementation of 'helm rollback'.
35	type Rollback struct {
36		cfg *Configuration
37	
38		Version       int
39		Timeout       time.Duration
40		Wait          bool
41		WaitForJobs   bool
42		DisableHooks  bool
43		DryRun        bool
44		Recreate      bool // will (if true) recreate pods after a rollback.
45		Force         bool // will (if true) force resource upgrade through uninstall/recreate if needed
46		CleanupOnFail bool
47		MaxHistory    int // MaxHistory limits the maximum number of revisions saved per release
48	}
49	
50	// NewRollback creates a new Rollback object with the given configuration.
51	func NewRollback(cfg *Configuration) *Rollback {
52		return &Rollback{
53			cfg: cfg,
54		}
55	}
56	
57	// Run executes 'helm rollback' against the given release.
58	func (r *Rollback) Run(name string) error {
59		if err := r.cfg.KubeClient.IsReachable(); err != nil {
60			return err
61		}
62	
63		r.cfg.Releases.MaxHistory = r.MaxHistory
64	
65		r.cfg.Log("preparing rollback of %s", name)
66		currentRelease, targetRelease, err := r.prepareRollback(name)
67		if err != nil {
68			return err
69		}
70	
71		if !r.DryRun {
72			r.cfg.Log("creating rolled back release for %s", name)
73			if err := r.cfg.Releases.Create(targetRelease); err != nil {
74				return err
75			}
76		}
77	
78		r.cfg.Log("performing rollback of %s", name)
79		if _, err := r.performRollback(currentRelease, targetRelease); err != nil {
80			return err
81		}
82	
83		if !r.DryRun {
84			r.cfg.Log("updating status for rolled back release for %s", name)
85			if err := r.cfg.Releases.Update(targetRelease); err != nil {
86				return err
87			}
88		}
89		return nil
90	}
91	
92	// prepareRollback finds the previous release and prepares a new release object with
93	// the previous release's configuration
94	func (r *Rollback) prepareRollback(name string) (*release.Release, *release.Release, error) {
95		if err := chartutil.ValidateReleaseName(name); err != nil {
96			return nil, nil, errors.Errorf("prepareRollback: Release name is invalid: %s", name)
97		}
98	
99		if r.Version < 0 {
100			return nil, nil, errInvalidRevision
101		}
102	
103		currentRelease, err := r.cfg.Releases.Last(name)
104		if err != nil {
105			return nil, nil, err
106		}
107	
108		previousVersion := r.Version
109		if r.Version == 0 {
110			previousVersion = currentRelease.Version - 1
111		}
112	
113		historyReleases, err := r.cfg.Releases.History(name)
114		if err != nil {
115			return nil, nil, err
116		}
117	
118		// Check if the history version to be rolled back exists
119		previousVersionExist := false
120		for _, historyRelease := range historyReleases {
121			version := historyRelease.Version
122			if previousVersion == version {
123				previousVersionExist = true
124				break
125			}
126		}
127		if !previousVersionExist {
128			return nil, nil, errors.Errorf("release has no %d version", previousVersion)
129		}
130	
131		r.cfg.Log("rolling back %s (current: v%d, target: v%d)", name, currentRelease.Version, previousVersion)
132	
133		previousRelease, err := r.cfg.Releases.Get(name, previousVersion)
134		if err != nil {
135			return nil, nil, err
136		}
137	
138		// Store a new release object with previous release's configuration
139		targetRelease := &release.Release{
140			Name:      name,
141			Namespace: currentRelease.Namespace,
142			Chart:     previousRelease.Chart,
143			Config:    previousRelease.Config,
144			Info: &release.Info{
145				FirstDeployed: currentRelease.Info.FirstDeployed,
146				LastDeployed:  helmtime.Now(),
147				Status:        release.StatusPendingRollback,
148				Notes:         previousRelease.Info.Notes,
149				// Because we lose the reference to previous version elsewhere, we set the
150				// message here, and only override it later if we experience failure.

## Comprehensive Analysis: Helm SDK Install/Upgrade/Uninstall Internal Operations

Based on my thorough examination of the Helm SDK source code in your project's vendor directory, here's a detailed breakdown of how the Helm SDK handles install/upgrade/uninstall operations, including the exact sequence of operations and the separability of the Wait operation.

---

### 1. INSTALL ACTION - `action.Install.Run()` / `action.Install.RunWithContext()`

**File:** `/Users/xuwentao/Projects/tele/paas-controller/vendor/helm.sh/helm/v3/pkg/action/install.go`

#### High-Level Sequence:
1. Cluster reachability check
2. Pre-install validations
3. CRD installation (if present)
4. Render chart templates
5. Build Kubernetes resources
6. **Store release record in storage** (line 402)
7. **Execute performInstall()** (line 409)
   - Pre-install hooks (line 448)
   - Create/Update resources in Kubernetes (line 457-463)
   - **Wait for resources** (line 469-478) - **CONDITIONAL & SEPARABLE**
   - Post-install hooks (line 481)
   - Mark as StatusDeployed (line 487-489)
   - Update release record (line 499)

#### Key Code Sections:

**Release Record Creation (Line 402):**
```go
// Store the release in history before continuing (new in Helm 3). We always know
// that this is a create operation.
if err := i.cfg.Releases.Create(rel); err != nil {
    // We could try to recover gracefully here, but since nothing has been installed
    // yet, this is probably safer than trying to continue when we know storage is
    // not working.
    return rel, err
}
```

**Wait Operation (Lines 469-478):**
```go
if i.Wait {
    if i.WaitForJobs {
        err = i.cfg.KubeClient.WaitWithJobs(resources, i.Timeout)
    } else {
        err = i.cfg.KubeClient.Wait(resources, i.Timeout)
    }
    if err != nil {
        return rel, err
    }
}
```

**Critical Insight:** The Wait is a **completely separable step**. The `i.Wait` boolean flag controls whether this block executes. The release record is already created before `performInstall()` is called, and resources are created before the wait block. This means you can:
- Create resources without waiting (set `Wait=false`)
- Perform waiting as a separate subsequent operation

---

### 2. UPGRADE ACTION - `action.Upgrade.RunWithContext()`

**File:** `/Users/xuwentao/Projects/tele/paas-controller/vendor/helm.sh/helm/v3/pkg/action/upgrade.go`

#### High-Level Sequence:
1. Cluster reachability check
2. `prepareUpgrade()` (line 165)
   - Load current release from storage
   - Validate manifest
   - Create new release object with StatusPendingUpgrade
   - **Does NOT create release record yet**
3. `performUpgrade()` (line 173)
   - Create release record (line 376) 
   - Launch `releasingUpgrade()` in goroutine (line 383)
   - Handle context cancellation
4. `releasingUpgrade()` (line 417)
   - Pre-upgrade hooks (line 421)
   - Update resources in Kubernetes (line 429)
   - Recreate pods if needed (line 436-443)
   - **Wait for resources** (line 446-462) - **CONDITIONAL & SEPARABLE**
   - Post-upgrade hooks (line 467)
   - Mark as StatusDeployed (line 476)
   - Record old release as StatusSuperseded (line 474)

#### Key Code Sections:

**Release Record Creation (Line 376):**
```go
u.cfg.Log("creating upgraded release for %s", upgradedRelease.Name)
if err := u.cfg.Releases.Create(upgradedRelease); err != nil {
    return nil, err
}
```

**Wait Operation (Lines 446-462):**
```go
if u.Wait {
    u.cfg.Log(
        "waiting for release %s resources (created: %d updated: %d  deleted: %d)",
        upgradedRelease.Name, len(results.Created), len(results.Updated), len(results.Deleted))
    if u.WaitForJobs {
        if err := u.cfg.KubeClient.WaitWithJobs(target, u.Timeout); err != nil {
            u.cfg.recordRelease(originalRelease)
            u.reportToPerformUpgrade(c, upgradedRelease, results.Created, err)
            return
        }
    } else {
        if err := u.cfg.KubeClient.Wait(target, u.Timeout); err != nil {
            u.cfg.recordRelease(originalRelease)

```

**Critical Insight:** Upgrade also supports a **separable Wait** controlled by the `u.Wait` flag. However, upgrade uses goroutines and channels, making it more complex than install. The release record is created early (line 376), and resources are updated (line 429) before waiting (line 446-462).

---

### 3. UNINSTALL ACTION - `action.Uninstall.Run()`

**File:** `/Users/xuwentao/Projects/tele/paas-controller/vendor/helm.sh/helm/v3/pkg/action/uninstall.go`

#### High-Level Sequence:
1. Cluster reachability check
2. Validate release name
3. Load release history
4. Pre-delete hooks (line 109)
5. Update release to StatusUninstalling (line 118)
6. Delete resources from Kubernetes (line 122)
7. **Wait for deletion** (line 133-139) - **CONDITIONAL & SEPARABLE**
8. Post-delete hooks (line 142)
9. Mark as StatusUninstalled (line 147)
10. Purge release if requested (line 154-159)

#### Key Code Sections:

**Wait for Deletion (Lines 133-139):**
```go
if u.Wait {
    if kubeClient, ok := u.cfg.KubeClient.(kube.InterfaceExt); ok {
        if err := kubeClient.WaitForDelete(deletedResources, u.Timeout); err != nil {
            errs = append(errs, err)
        }
    }
}
```

**Critical Insight:** Uninstall's wait is the **most clearly separable**. It's a simple conditional block that waits for resources to be actually deleted from Kubernetes. The release record is marked as uninstalled regardless of whether wait succeeds.

---

### 4. APISERVER HELM HANDLER INTEGRATION

**Files:**
- `/Users/xuwentao/Projects/tele/paas-controller/pkg/apiserver/helm/helm_release.go` (HTTP handlers)
- `/Users/xuwentao/Projects/tele/paas-controller/pkg/apiserver/helm/controller/helm_release.go` (Controller)
- `/Users/xuwentao/Projects/tele/paas-controller/pkg/helm/releases.go` (HelmClient implementation)

#### How the APIServer Calls the Helm SDK:

**Install (Line 90-142 in releases.go):**
```go
func (c *HelmClient) Install(installReleaseRequest *models.InstallOrUpgradeReleaseRequest) (*release.Release, error) {
    // ... setup ...
    installClient := action.NewInstall(hc)
    installClient.Wait = installReleaseRequest.Wait  // <-- RESPECTS THE Wait FLAG
    // ... more configuration ...
    rel, err := installClient.Run(chart, values)
    // ...
}
```

**Upgrade (Line 220-266 in releases.go):**
```go
func (c *HelmClient) Upgrade(upgradeReleaseRequest *models.InstallOrUpgradeReleaseRequest) (*release.Release, error) {
    // ... setup ...
    upgradeClient := action.NewUpgrade(hc)
    upgradeClient.Wait = upgradeReleaseRequest.Wait  // <-- RESPECTS THE Wait FLAG
    // ... more configuration ...
    rel, err := upgradeClient.Run(name, chart, values)
    // ...
}
```

**Uninstall (Line 205-218 in releases.go):**
```go
func (c *HelmClient) Uninstall(namespace string, name string) error {
    // ... setup ...
    uninstallClient := action.NewUninstall(hc)
    // NOTE: Wait is NOT being set here - defaults to false!
    _, err = uninstallClient.Run(name)
    // ...
}
```

**CRITICAL DISCOVERY:** The uninstall handler **does not set the Wait flag** at all! This means uninstalls always proceed without waiting for resource deletion.

---

### 5. THE WAIT MECHANISM - `kube.Wait()` and `kube.WaitForDelete()`

**File:** `/Users/xuwentao/Projects/tele/paas-controller/vendor/helm.sh/helm/v3/pkg/kube/wait.go`

#### How Wait Works:

**waitForResources() (Lines 49-91):**
```go
func (w *waiter) waitForResources(created ResourceList) error {
    w.log("beginning wait for %d resources with timeout of %v", len(created), w.timeout)
    
    startTime := time.Now()
    ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), w.timeout)
    defer cancel()
    
    // Poll every 2 seconds until all resources are ready
    err := wait.PollUntilContextCancel(ctx, 2*time.Second, true, func(ctx context.Context) (bool, error) {
        // Check IsReady() status for each resource
        // Retry up to 30 times on transient errors
        // Return true when all resources are ready
    })
    
    return err
}
```

**waitForDeletedResources() (Lines 113-128):**
```go
func (w *waiter) waitForDeletedResources(deleted ResourceList) error {
    w.log("beginning wait for %d resources to be deleted with timeout of %v", len(deleted), w.timeout)
    
    ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), w.timeout)
    defer cancel()
    
    return wait.PollUntilContextCancel(ctx, 2*time.Second, true, func(_ context.Context) (bool, error) {
        for _, v := range deleted {
            err := v.Get()
            if err == nil || !apierrors.IsNotFound(err) {
                return false, err
            }
        }
        return true, nil
    })
}
```

**Interface Definition (Lines 35-40 in interface.go):**
```go
// Wait waits up to the given timeout for the specified resources to be ready.
Wait(resources ResourceList, timeout time.Duration) error

// WaitWithJobs wait up to the given timeout for the specified resources to be ready, including jobs.
WaitWithJobs(resources ResourceList, timeout time.Duration) error
```

**Key Properties:**
- Polling interval: **2 seconds**
- Max retries on transient errors: **30**
- Timeout: Configurable (default in apiserver: 5 minutes for install)
- **Completely independent** of resource creation/updating
- **Returns immediately on completion** or timeout

---

### 6. WAIT SEPARABILITY ANALYSIS

#### Can Wait be Split Out?

**YES - ABSOLUTELY.** The evidence:

1. **Install:** The release record is created BEFORE `performInstall()`. The Wait operation is a simple conditional block inside `performInstall()` that comes AFTER resource creation.

2. **Upgrade:** Similar to install - release record created first, then resources updated, then wait is optional.

3. **Uninstall:** Resources are deleted, and then waiting for deletion is optional.

4. **Design Pattern:** All three actions use a simple boolean flag (`Wait`) to control whether waiting happens. This is explicitly designed for split operations.

5. **Architectural Consequence:** You can:
   - Call Install/Upgrade with `Wait=false`, get the release immediately
   - Poll the resources separately using any status-checking mechanism
   - Implement async operations where the HTTP response returns before resources are ready

#### Current APIServer Behavior:

Looking at `/Users/xuwentao/Projects/tele/paas-controller/pkg/apiserver/helm/helm_release.go`:

- **InstallRelease (line 71):** Respects `installReleaseRequest.Wait`
- **UpgradeRelease (line 107):** Respects `upgradeReleaseRequest.Wait`
- **UninstallRelease (line 86):** **No Wait configuration** - always synchronous delete without waiting

---

### 7. RELEASE RECORD LIFECYCLE

#### Install:
```
1. Release object created in memory (line 314 in install.go)
2. Manifest rendered and stored (line 317)
3. Release status set to StatusPendingInstall (line 330)
4. Release record created in storage (line 402) ← CRITICAL POINT
5. Resources created in Kubernetes (line 457-463)
6. Resources polled for readiness (line 469-478, if Wait=true)
7. Release status updated to StatusDeployed (line 487-489)
8. Release record updated in storage (line 499)
```

#### Upgrade:
```
1. Prepare phase: new release object created with StatusPendingUpgrade (line 286-305)
2. Release record created in storage (line 376) ← CRITICAL POINT
3. Resources updated in Kubernetes (line 429)
4. Resources polled for readiness (line 446-462, if Wait=true)
5. Release status updated to StatusDeployed (line 476)
6. Old release marked as StatusSuperseded (line 473)
7. Release records updated in storage (line 181-182 in RunWithContext)
```

#### Uninstall:
```
1. Release status set to StatusUninstalling (line 103)
2. Release record updated in storage (line 118)
3. Resources deleted from Kubernetes (line 122)
4. Deleted resources polled (line 135, if Wait=true)
5. Release status set to StatusUninstalled (line 147)
6. Release record updated in storage (line 169 or 156)
```

---

### 8. KEY FINDINGS & RECOMMENDATIONS

| Aspect | Install | Upgrade | Uninstall |
|--------|---------|---------|-----------|
| **Release Record Created** | Before resource creation (line 402) | Before resource update (line 376) | Before resource deletion (line 118) |
| **Resources Applied** | Create (line 457) | Update (line 429) | Delete (line 122) |
| **Wait Implemented** | Conditional block (line 469-478) | Conditional block (line 446-462) | Conditional block (line 135) |
| **Wait Separable** | YES | YES | YES |
| **APIServer Sets Wait** | YES (from request) | YES (from request) | NO (hardcoded false) |
| **Wait Implementation** | `kube.Wait()` | `kube.Wait()` | `kube.WaitForDelete()` |
| **Polling Interval** | 2 seconds | 2 seconds | 2 seconds |
| **Default Timeout** | 5 minutes | From request | Not set |

### Conclusion:

The Helm SDK is **architecturally designed** to support separated wait operations. All three actions (install, upgrade, uninstall) store the release record early and treat the wait as a distinct, optional phase. This is perfect for implementing async operations in your PaaS controller—simply pass `Wait=false` to get immediate responses, then implement separate polling or webhook mechanisms for users to check deployment status.
